title: Loosely Coupled Connections

Loosely Coupled Connections

Loosely Coupled Connections

Components are connected by the operations they expose to each other, and these connections can be loosely or tightly coupled. Components are loosely coupled if you can make changes to the internal structure of a component without having to make changes to the components with which it communicates. Components are tightly coupled if changes to one component require that changes also be made to related components.

Loosely coupled connections are a good practice in component-based development in general, and this is particularly true for three-tier development. Loosely coupled connections give great flexibility to the architecture and implementation of each tier.

A loosely coupled connection is defined solely by the data interchanged between the components, and is independent of the internal state or structure of the components themselves. Thus, the structure of the data streams between components is the crucial consideration when building for loosely coupled connections:

  • If the data stream can be described rigorously, then a component can declare its data requirements to the outside world.

    For example, XML uses DTDs for structural definitions. Components that use XML to exchange data can declare that they can process data conforming to a given DTD, and other components can produce data targeted at the DTD without requiring any knowledge of the target component.

  • If the stream can be processed and transformed easily, then the data in the stream can be loaded into the component regardless of how the structure of the stream or the component changes.

    XML and JSON are supported by other technologies and standards, that enable transformation into a wide range of output formats.

  • If the stream can be processed by generally available, reusable software, then the stream can be passed to other components, such as .Net and COM components.

    Third-party XML parsers and processors are readily available on most infrastructures.

Related Links
  • XML Streams

    XML streams are data streams using an XML format. They enable loosely coupled connections between components, and provide a standardized format for data transport between components. Uniface can transfer field data and data relationships via XML stream parameters. It can also attach processing information to data in an XML stream to allow the data state to be transported with the data.
